CASE STUDY 01

Urbanese

Urban Lifestyle & Community Platform

UX ResearchApp DesignFigmaPrototypingUser Testing

Problem

Urban communities lacked a single platform to discover local events, connect with neighbours, and navigate city life. Existing apps were either too broad or too niche, leaving urban users fragmented across multiple tools.

Research & Process

Conducted user interviews with 15 urban residents aged 20–35. Mapped pain points around event discovery, local community trust, and information overload. Created personas, journey maps, and competitive analysis before moving to wireframes.

Solution & Design

Designed a community-first app with a hyperlocal feed, event discovery engine, and neighbour connection features. Built an intuitive IA with minimal friction from discovery to action. Delivered high-fidelity Figma prototype with complete design system.

Outcome

Prototype tested with 10 users — 90% task completion rate. Users reported the navigation felt "intuitive" and the community feed was "the feature they didn't know they needed."

CASE STUDY 02

Smagger

Social & Utility Campus Platform

Social PlatformUI DesignUser TestingFigmaDesign System

Problem

Campus students struggled to stay connected, share resources, and coordinate activities through fragmented tools like WhatsApp groups, notice boards, and email. There was no unified platform designed specifically for the campus context.

Research & Process

Surveyed 40+ students across MIT WPU. Identified three core needs — social connection, academic resource sharing, and event coordination. Benchmarked against existing social and utility apps to find the design gap.

Solution & Design

Built a dual-mode app — social feed for connection and utility hub for resources and events. Created a scalable design system with reusable components, ensuring consistency across 30+ screens. Full Figma prototype with interaction design.

Outcome

Approved as UX Studio assignment by professor. Test users described it as "the app our college actually needs" — validating both the problem definition and the design solution.

CASE STUDY 03

Kita-Bae

Book Discovery & Reading App

Reading AppInformation ArchitectureUI DesignFigmaInteraction Design

Problem

Book lovers found it frustrating to discover the right next read. Existing platforms were cluttered, recommendation algorithms felt generic, and the browsing experience lacked the serendipity of a real bookshop.

Research & Process

Interviewed 12 regular readers. Key insight: people want to "stumble" on great books, not search for them. Mapped the emotional journey of book discovery and identified where digital apps fail to recreate the bookshop feeling.

Solution & Design

Designed a discovery-first reading app with mood-based browsing, curated shelves, and a minimal reading tracker. Focused on delight — smooth animations, tactile card interactions, and a warm visual language that feels like holding a real book.

Outcome

User testing showed 85% of participants discovered at least one book they "genuinely wanted to read" within the first 3 minutes. The mood filter was rated the most unique and useful feature.

What I Do

Services That
Deliver Results

Every project is designed to solve a real problem and grow your business — not just look good.

01
🌐
Web Design

Custom websites for gyms, schools, clinics and local businesses. Mobile friendly, Google optimized, with WhatsApp and enquiry forms built in. Your competitor’s website won’t stand a chance.

02
🧩
Design System

Scalable, consistent design systems with reusable components, typography, color tokens, and guidelines. Built in Figma — developer handoff ready. Your product stays consistent as it grows.

03
🚀
MVP Design

End-to-end design for your startup MVP — from research and wireframes to high-fidelity UI and prototype. Fast, focused, built to validate your idea and impress investors.

04
🔍
User Research

In-depth user interviews, surveys, journey mapping, persona creation, and usability testing. Understand your users deeply before building — so every design decision is backed by real insight.

About Me

DESIGNER.
THINKER.
BUILDER.

I’m Sarthak Waghmare — a UX Designer at MIT World Peace University, Pune. I think like a strategist, design like a craftsman, and ship work that actually solves problems.

I believe great design is invisible — it just works. I’ve competed at IIT Guwahati and the Design Fusion Hackathon, and shipped 3 end-to-end app case studies. I bring research, systems thinking, and sharp UI together.
